paper_evaluations.title: Parameters of Spinal Cord Stimulation in Complex Regional Pain Syndrome: Systematic Review and Meta-analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials.

1.reason: Title explicitly identifies the report as a 'Systematic Review and Meta-analysis'.

2.reason: Structured abstract is provided with Background, Objective, Study Design, Methods, Results, Limitations, and Conclusion sections.

3.reason: Introduction describes the gap: no prior review of SCS parameters for CRPS in RCTs, establishing rationale in context of existing knowledge.

4.reason: Three explicit objectives are stated: characterize SCS waveforms/parameters and outcomes, determine preferred/most effective waveform, and provide narrative review.

5.reason: Inclusion/exclusion criteria are provided but there is no explicit description of how studies were grouped for the syntheses.

6.reason: Three databases (PubMed, Embase, CINHLA) are named, but no search dates, registers, websites, or reference lists are specified.

7.reason: Only general keywords are mentioned; full search strategies for each database with filters and limits are not presented.

8.reason: No description of the screening process, number of reviewers involved, whether they worked independently, or use of automation tools.

9.reason: Data collection methods are not described; number of reviewers, independence, and processes for confirming data are not reported.

10a.reason: Outcomes are vaguely defined as 'pain relief' via VAS and GPE with 'other pain measurement data' considered; full specification of all outcome domains, measures, and time points is missing.

10b.reason: Other variables sought are only partially listed (patient numbers, treatment modalities, SCS parameters); no comprehensive list or assumptions about missing information are described.

11.reason: Cochrane RoB 2 tool specified; two independent reviewers assessed each study; disagreements resolved by discussion or third author.

12.reason: Weighted mean difference (MD) with 95% CI is specified as the effect measure for pain scores and GPE.

13a.reason: No process is described for determining which studies were eligible for each synthesis (e.g., comparing intervention characteristics to planned groups).

13b.reason: No methods for handling missing summary statistics or performing data conversions are described.

13c.reason: No explicit description of methods used to tabulate or visually display results; RevMan 5.4 is mentioned only in the context of analysis.

13d.reason: Random-effects model (DerSimonian and Laird) and software (RevMan 5.4) are named, but no method for identifying or measuring statistical heterogeneity is described.

13e.reason: No methods for exploring causes of heterogeneity (e.g., subgroup analysis, meta-regression) are described; only sensitivity analysis is mentioned.

13f.reason: Sensitivity analysis by stepwise exclusion of individual studies is described in the Methods section.

14.reason: No methods for assessing risk of bias due to missing results (e.g., funnel plots, Egger's test) are described.

15.reason: No methods for assessing certainty in the body of evidence (e.g., GRADE) are described.

16a.reason: Search results are described from 99 records to 4 included studies, with reference to a PRISMA flow diagram (Fig. 1).

16b.reason: No studies excluded after full-text review are cited individually with reasons for exclusion; only aggregate numbers are provided.

17.reason: All four included studies are cited and their characteristics are presented in Table 1 and described in the text.

18.reason: Risk of bias assessments for each included study are presented with reference to Fig. 2, describing domain-level results per study.

19.reason: Forest plots (Figs. 3, 4) present effect estimates and confidence intervals for each study; summary statistics are referenced though details are in figures.

20a.reason: Characteristics and risk of bias among studies contributing to each synthesis are not briefly summarized for each synthesis.

20b.reason: Meta-analysis results are presented with summary estimates (MD), 95% CIs, P values, and I² for both pain scores and GPE syntheses.

20c.reason: No investigations of possible causes of heterogeneity (subgroup analysis, meta-regression) were conducted or presented.

20d.reason: Sensitivity analysis results are presented in Table 2 and Figure 4, showing no significant change in the pooled estimate.

21.reason: No assessment of risk of bias due to missing results (reporting biases) is presented for any synthesis.

22.reason: No certainty of evidence assessment (e.g., GRADE) is presented for any outcome.

23a.reason: Discussion interprets results in context of other evidence, referencing prior studies and reviews on SCS for CRPS and neuropathic pain.

23b.reason: Limitations of the evidence are discussed, including mixing of pain pathologies, limited longitudinal data, and small CRPS patient subsets.

23c.reason: Review process limitations are discussed, including restricting search to RCTs and how this may have affected data curation.

23d.reason: Implications for future research are discussed, including recommendations for pathology-specific stratification and longer follow-up periods.

24a.reason: No registration information, register name, or registration number is provided, nor is it stated that the review was not registered.

24b.reason: No mention of a review protocol or where one could be accessed; it is not stated that a protocol was not prepared.

24c.reason: No registration or protocol is cited, so no amendments to such information are described.

25.reason: Disclaimer states 'There was no external funding in the preparation of this manuscript,' describing financial support.

26.reason: Competing interests are explicitly declared: each author certifies no commercial association that might pose a conflict of interest.

27.reason: No statement about public availability of data collection forms, extracted data, analytic code, or other materials is provided.

item_1.reason: Title explicitly identifies the report as a 'Systematic Review and Meta-analysis.'

item_2.reason: Abstract states objectives: 'To provide evidence for the use of SCS to treat CRPS and characterize the additional benefits of various SCS waveforms.'

item_3.reason: Only inclusion criteria are implied (RCTs comparing SCS parameters for CRPS); exclusion criteria are not explicitly specified.

item_4.reason: Databases (PubMed, Embase, CINHLA) are named but the date when each was last searched is not provided.

item_5.reason: Risk of bias ratings are reported but the specific method or tool used to assess risk of bias is not stated.

item_6.reason: Synthesis output metrics (MD, CI, I², P) are presented but the specific synthesis methods (e.g., fixed- vs random-effects model) are not described.

item_7.reason: Number of included studies (4) is given but the total number of participants across studies is not reported.

item_8.reason: Number of studies per analysis is reported and direction of effect is indicated, but number of participants for each outcome is not provided.

item_9.reason: Limitations are briefly summarized: one study at high risk of bias, some studies included mixed neuropathic pain populations, and insufficient evidence for novel SCS parameters.

item_10.reason: Interpretation and implications are provided: 'LF-SCS is superior to conventional therapy/placebo SCS... more evidence is required to demonstrate that novel SCS parameters are superior.'

item_11.reason: No primary funding source is mentioned anywhere in the abstract.

item_12.reason: No register name or registration number is provided in the abstract.

comparison_details.title: Parameters of Spinal Cord Stimulation in Complex Regional Pain Syndrome: Systematic Review and Meta-analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials.

items.ai_reason: Title explicitly identifies the report as a 'Systematic Review and Meta-analysis'.
Introduction describes the gap: no prior review of SCS parameters for CRPS in RCTs, establishing rationale in context of existing knowledge.
Three explicit objectives are stated: characterize SCS waveforms/parameters and outcomes, determine preferred/most effective waveform, and provide narrative review.
Inclusion/exclusion criteria are provided but there is no explicit description of how studies were grouped for the syntheses.
Three databases (PubMed, Embase, CINHLA) are named, but no search dates, registers, websites, or reference lists are specified.
Only general keywords are mentioned; full search strategies for each database with filters and limits are not presented.
No description of the screening process, number of reviewers involved, whether they worked independently, or use of automation tools.
Data collection methods are not described; number of reviewers, independence, and processes for confirming data are not reported.
Outcomes are vaguely defined as 'pain relief' via VAS and GPE with 'other pain measurement data' considered; full specification of all outcome domains, measures, and time points is missing.
Other variables sought are only partially listed (patient numbers, treatment modalities, SCS parameters); no comprehensive list or assumptions about missing information are described.
Cochrane RoB 2 tool specified; two independent reviewers assessed each study; disagreements resolved by discussion or third author.
Weighted mean difference (MD) with 95% CI is specified as the effect measure for pain scores and GPE.
No process is described for determining which studies were eligible for each synthesis (e.g., comparing intervention characteristics to planned groups).
No methods for handling missing summary statistics or performing data conversions are described.
No explicit description of methods used to tabulate or visually display results; RevMan 5.4 is mentioned only in the context of analysis.
Random-effects model (DerSimonian and Laird) and software (RevMan 5.4) are named, but no method for identifying or measuring statistical heterogeneity is described.
No methods for exploring causes of heterogeneity (e.g., subgroup analysis, meta-regression) are described; only sensitivity analysis is mentioned.
Sensitivity analysis by stepwise exclusion of individual studies is described in the Methods section.
No methods for assessing risk of bias due to missing results (e.g., funnel plots, Egger's test) are described.
No methods for assessing certainty in the body of evidence (e.g., GRADE) are described.
Search results are described from 99 records to 4 included studies, with reference to a PRISMA flow diagram (Fig. 1).
No studies excluded after full-text review are cited individually with reasons for exclusion; only aggregate numbers are provided.
All four included studies are cited and their characteristics are presented in Table 1 and described in the text.
Risk of bias assessments for each included study are presented with reference to Fig. 2, describing domain-level results per study.
Forest plots (Figs. 3, 4) present effect estimates and confidence intervals for each study; summary statistics are referenced though details are in figures.
Characteristics and risk of bias among studies contributing to each synthesis are not briefly summarized for each synthesis.
Meta-analysis results are presented with summary estimates (MD), 95% CIs, P values, and I² for both pain scores and GPE syntheses.
No investigations of possible causes of heterogeneity (subgroup analysis, meta-regression) were conducted or presented.
Sensitivity analysis results are presented in Table 2 and Figure 4, showing no significant change in the pooled estimate.
No assessment of risk of bias due to missing results (reporting biases) is presented for any synthesis.
No certainty of evidence assessment (e.g., GRADE) is presented for any outcome.
Discussion interprets results in context of other evidence, referencing prior studies and reviews on SCS for CRPS and neuropathic pain.
Limitations of the evidence are discussed, including mixing of pain pathologies, limited longitudinal data, and small CRPS patient subsets.
Review process limitations are discussed, including restricting search to RCTs and how this may have affected data curation.
Implications for future research are discussed, including recommendations for pathology-specific stratification and longer follow-up periods.
No registration information, register name, or registration number is provided, nor is it stated that the review was not registered.
No mention of a review protocol or where one could be accessed; it is not stated that a protocol was not prepared.
No registration or protocol is cited, so no amendments to such information are described.
Disclaimer states 'There was no external funding in the preparation of this manuscript,' describing financial support.
Competing interests are explicitly declared: each author certifies no commercial association that might pose a conflict of interest.
No statement about public availability of data collection forms, extracted data, analytic code, or other materials is provided.
